psychology_notes: Particles = juice. Particles = celebration. Particles = impact. They communicate energy.
reasoning:
1. **Flow**: Particle system created with capacity. Emit batches of particles with random initial velocities. Per-frame update positions/lifetimes. Dead particles recycled.

2. **Math**: Capacity buffer pre-allocated to avoid GC. Recycling avoids alloc churn.

3. **Edge cases**: Tab background (no requestAnimationFrame) — pause emitter, particles freeze. Low-end GPU — auto-detect, reduce capacity.

4. **Visual**: Configurable per-effect (burst, fountain, sparks). Lifetime curves create natural fades.

5. **Anti-exploit**: Cosmetic only.

6. **Rejected**: CPU particle loop (low cap), individual Sprite per particle (drawcall explosion).

database_design.schema: N/A — runtime.

visualization_strategy: Particle color lerp creates 'cooling embers' effect (start orange, end red). Size shrinks for fade. Additive blending makes overlapping particles brighter — explosion feel. Reduced motion: emit fewer (0.2x count) or disable entirely. Color-blind: effect distinguishability via shape, not just color.
balance_considerations: Watch FPS by device — auto-detect and reduce capacity. Visual quality A/B test (more vs fewer particles).
exploit_vectors_addressed: GPU resource exhaustion via spam emission — capacity hard limit.
observability: Log FPS, particle count active. Metrics: avg frame time, capacity utilization.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior loops 10k particles in CPU — 5fps. Then: individual Sprite each — drawcall explosion, GPU bottleneck. Then: no recycling — GC stutters every emit.
real_world_inspiration: DONE WELL: Beat Saber particles, three.js examples. DONE BADLY: many indie web games with copy-paste particle code that tanks performance.

game_genre_context: Online competitive game with rating-based ranked mode. Boosting (paying for high-rank account) and account-sharing destroy ranked integrity. Behavioral detection is the only effective countermeasure.
title: Behavioral Biometrics for Account-Sharing and Boost Detection
design_intent: Build a behavioral fingerprinting system that learns player's mouse/keyboard biometric pattern (acceleration curves, click timing) and flags when behavior changes drastically (account hijack OR account-sharing for boosting). Riot's anti-boost team uses this. Banning sold accounts kills the booster economy.
mathematical_foundation: Feature vector per session: mean+std of mouse acceleration in 8 direction bins, click-to-click time distributions, keypress hold durations, action-per-minute curve. Distance metric: Mahalanobis from established player baseline. Threshold: distance > 4σ triggers review.
psychology_notes: Boosting destroys ranked. 'I'm Diamond rank' identity collapses when player face an obvious smurf-boost in their lobby. Catching boosters preserves ladder integrity.
reasoning:
1. **Flow**: Each session, collect behavioral telemetry. Update player's baseline incrementally. Compute distance per session. Spike triggers shadow-suspicious flag, manual review queue.

2. **Math**: Mahalanobis distance accounts for covariance — better than Euclidean.

3. **Edge cases**: Player upgraded mouse (legit change) — baseline retrains. Player playing tilted (legit variance) — within distribution. Boosting: dramatically different acceleration profile.

4. **Visual**: No player UI. Suspicious accounts handled by review team.

5. **Anti-exploit**: Boosters can train similar profiles, but at lab-level — economically inefficient.

6. **Rejected**: IP-based detection (VPNs bypass), session-only check (misses sustained sharing).

database_design.schema: player_biometric: player_id, baseline_vector NUMERIC[50], baseline_covariance NUMERIC[][], session_count INT, last_updated. session_biometric: id, player_id, session_id, vector NUMERIC[50], distance_from_baseline NUMERIC.

visualization_strategy: No player-facing UI. Silent system. Reduced motion: N/A.
balance_considerations: Watch false positive rate from manual review (target <0.5%). Watch boost-detection precision. Watch baseline retrain rate (every 30 sessions).
exploit_vectors_addressed: Account boosting — distinct biometric.
Account sharing — distinct biometric.
Account hijacking — distinct biometric (login from new device).
Hardware change masking — baseline retrains gradually, sudden change still flagged.
observability: Log every session biometric, distance, flags. Metrics: distance distribution, flag rate.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: IP-only detection — VPN bypass. Then: no behavior tracking — boosting market thrives. Riot's anti-boost was a years-long project; behavioral biometrics are the breakthrough.
real_world_inspiration: DONE WELL: Riot's anti-boost system, Valve's VAC behavior signals. DONE BADLY: most games rely on player reports only — boosters operate openly until reported.
